title: Fix ElevenLabs TTS 'request canceled' toast on replay/backtrack

source.notes: User report: navigating backward over a move and replaying TTS triggers a yellow toast 'ElevenLabs TTS failed TypeError: request canceled'. Voice continues working but toast is disruptive.

description: When the user backtracks or replays a move, stopSpeaking() aborts the in-flight ElevenLabs fetch via AbortController. In Tauri's webview, the aborted fetch throws TypeError('request canceled') instead of the standard DOMException with name='AbortError'. The catch block at tts.ts:1324 only checks for DOMException/AbortError, so the TypeError falls through to the cloud provider error handler at line 1345 which shows the toast. The fix: broaden the abort detection to also catch TypeError with 'cancel' in the message.
notes: Root cause: tts.ts:1324 — `if (e instanceof DOMException && e.name === 'AbortError') return;`
Tauri webview throws TypeError('request canceled') not DOMException('AbortError')
This also affects the timeout path at line 1277 (15s abort) — same error type
The toast text is: 'ElevenLabs TTS failed' / 'TypeError: request canceled'
Audio from cache works fine — the error only happens on in-flight API requests

in_scope: Broaden abort detection in the catch block at tts.ts:1322-1324
Silently return on any abort/cancel-related error, not just DOMException
Also handle the 'Failed to fetch' variant that some webviews throw on abort
out_of_scope: Google Cloud TTS (uses same abort pattern but may have same issue — fix both)

requirements: No toast appears when backtracking over a narrated move
No toast appears when rapidly clicking through moves during auto-narrate
Real errors (bad API key, network down, rate limit) still show toasts
Timeout errors (15s) still show the timeout-specific toast message
edge_cases: Rapid forward/backward navigation — should never show cancel toasts
Navigate away mid-sentence — should stop cleanly, no toast
Network actually drops during TTS — should still show error toast
Invalid API key — should still show error toast

acceptance_gates: No toast on backtrack/replay with valid ElevenLabs key
Toast still appears for genuine errors (bad key, network failure)

manual: Enable ElevenLabs TTS with valid API key and auto-narrate on
Navigate through 5+ moves, then backtrack 3 moves — NO cancel toasts should appear
Rapidly click forward/backward through moves — NO cancel toasts
Click the speaker icon on a move to replay — should play without toast
Set an invalid API key, try narration — SHOULD show error toast (not 'request canceled')
Disconnect network, try narration — SHOULD show timeout/network error toast

done_criteria: Abort/cancel errors are silently swallowed (no toast)
Real API errors still produce user-visible toasts
Timeout errors still produce the 'timed out' toast
Manual testing confirms no spurious toasts on backtrack/replay
